title: Geometric sizing — sequence to all-in by river (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective)
bodyMarkdown:
To get all-in by the river with N remaining streets at a constant fraction-of-pot sizing, the geometric sequence is: bet fraction f satisfies (1 + 2f)^N = effectiveStack / pot.

Solve for f given N streets remaining:
- 3 streets (flop, turn, river): f = ((SPR + 1)^(1/3) - 1) / 2.
- 2 streets (turn, river): f = ((SPR + 1)^(1/2) - 1) / 2.
- 1 street (river): f = SPR / 2 (jam).

Worked examples (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective):
- SPR 5 over 3 streets: f ≈ 0.40 (40% pot bet each street).
- SPR 8 over 3 streets: f ≈ 0.54 (54% pot bet each street).
- SPR 13 over 2 streets: f ≈ 0.87 (87% pot bet each remaining street).

This is the geometric default; sizing tells, range advantage, and texture pull from this baseline.
theoryCitation: POKER_THEORY.md §3 (Postflop Theory) — geometric sizing baseline. Phase 1 derivation per project_rake_spr_antes (2026-03-29).

assumptions.field: all 9-handed live cash and tournament fields; geometric formula is field-invariant

atomicityJustification: Single derivation (geometric sizing across streets) presented as one card.
